evals.prompt: Run /z-eval-judge on a llm.yml where one case has verbosity 3.8 and another has confidence 0.22.
evals.expected_output: The judge writes a judge: block with at least one finding per anomaly, each with severity warn or flag, and at least one recommendation. The original totals and aggregates are preserved.
assertions: llm.yml has a top-level judge: block appended
At least one finding flags verbosity > 2.0 for the high-verbosity case
At least one finding flags confidence < 0.4 for the low-confidence case
The original totals block is unchanged
The original aggregates block is unchanged

evals.prompt: Run /z-eval-judge on a llm.yml where brittle-case lists several `contains` graders whose `matched_token` needles are only two characters each (loose substring checks).
evals.expected_output: The judge flags those grader rows, ties recommendations to regex or llm-judge style scorers, and surfaces a structured needs_user_input hand-off toward /z-eval-update instead of editing eval sources inline.
assertions: At least one finding has dimension: grader for each flagged case
The detail on each grader finding quotes each matched_token needle (or equivalent quoted substring) and ties it to fewer than four characters of meaningful evidence
Recommendations contrast loose `contains`/`matched_token` substring checks with regex matchers or llm-judge scorers instead of vague "edit eval definitions" wording alone
needs_user_input (or equivalent structured questions/options) routes remediation through /z-eval-update without silent edits to eval registrations
No evals.json files were modified by this skill directly

evals.expected_output: The skill aborts cleanly, pointing the user to /z-eval-execute.
assertions: No files were created or modified
The skill printed a pointer to /z-eval-execute
The skill did not crash

evals.prompt: After the latest eval finished, I want the Judge Evals skill loaded from `/z-eval-judge`. Take the newest `{evalsDir}/_runs/…` bundle, read `static.yml`, `llm.yml`, `report.yml`, and the per-case logs before you change anything. Grade assertion reachability against the grader notes, call out brittle `contains` checks shorter than four characters, and surface soft-metric problems like verbosity far from one, confidence under 0.4, accuracy under 0.5, or wall-clock duration past two standard deviations from the suite mean. Append only a `judge` block to `llm.yml` that records the configured judge model, ISO timestamp, findings, and concrete `/z-eval-update` recommendations—leave `totals` and `aggregates` untouched, do not re-run cases, and do not edit eval bundle JSON by hand. When automation is sensible, close with the documented `needs_user_input` object (handoff / accept-all / walk / no) so the command can resume; keep askQuestion out of the skill thread itself.
assertions: The assistant resolves the lexicographically or timestamp-newest child directory under `{evalsDir}/_runs/` before citing any per-case metrics.
`static.yml`, `llm.yml`, `report.yml`, and the relevant `logs/<case>.log` files are read or explicitly referenced prior to proposing writes.

The critique covers mapping between case assertions and grader output, questions `contains` matchers under four characters, and highlights abnormal verbosity, low confidence, low accuracy, or duration spikes beyond two sigma from the suite average.
The write plan appends a `judge` map to `llm.yml` that names the active `judgeModel` and ISO `analysed_at` while leaving pre-existing `totals` and `aggregates` blocks unchanged.
Closing guidance routes remediation through `/z-eval-update` instead of direct JSON surgery, and never describes re-invoking the eval runner.

When follow-up is warranted the assistant emits structured `needs_user_input` with the documented reason, question id, and accept-all / walk / no choices, without invoking askQuestion from the judging skill.

primitive_analysis.summary: The skill post-processes the latest eval run tree, appends a judge section to llm.yml without rewriting rollups, and escalates follow-up through structured needs_user_input so the palette command owns askQuestion.

evals.expected_output: Narrative opens with which `_runs` directory was reviewed, lists findings across assertion, grader, verbosity, accuracy, confidence, and timing dimensions, shows an appended `judge` section plan that respects existing rollups, and finishes with the structured hand-off when updates are suggested.

evals.prompt: Judge Evals is supposed to run now that configuration exists, but the evaluator has not created any timestamped directories under `_runs/` yet. Follow Step 1 and tell me what I should do next in the palette.
assertions: The assistant names `/z-eval-execute` as the next palette action rather than inventing phantom run artefacts.
No `judge` append or other rewrite of `llm.yml` is described while `_runs/` lacks timestamped session folders.

primitive_analysis.summary: The skill post-processes the latest eval run tree, appends a judge section to llm.yml without rewriting rollups, and escalates follow-up through structured needs_user_input so the palette command owns askQuestion.
fixture_justifications: Pins `evalsDir` to `evals` beside the sandbox root so lookups stay deterministic.
Provides an `_runs/` container without hourly run subfolders so Step 1’s empty-state behaviour is exercised.

files.content: Holds the directory open until the first stamped run folder appears.

evals.expected_output: The assistant declines to synthesise judge output, points the operator toward `/z-eval-execute`, and does not describe mutating `llm.yml`.
